display.purpose: Hero and section word-stacks only; solid line + outlined line (Stencil Rule).

label.purpose: HUD furniture: eyebrows, counters, coordinates, terminal — the machine's voice.

shadows.name: cta-glow
shadows.purpose: Teal halo under primary buttons at rest.

shadows.name: cta-glow-hover
shadows.purpose: Intensified halo on primary button hover.

shadows.name: ambient-bloom
shadows.purpose: Free-standing glow behind hero photo, under anchors and counters.

shadows.name: nav-lift
shadows.purpose: The only conventional drop shadow — condensed floating navbar on scroll.

motion.name: ease-out-cubic
motion.purpose: Default easing for nav condense, state transitions.

motion.name: cta-transition
motion.purpose: Primary/ghost button lift + glow on hover.

motion.name: scan-sweep
motion.purpose: 1px accent bar sweeping top→bottom across HUD viewports.

extensions.motionDoctrine: Motion is intentionally always-on; this system ignores prefers-reduced-motion by design (see ThemeContext.jsx and the About terminal). Contrast and hierarchy are engineered to hold up under constant motion.

components.name: Primary CTA
components.description: Pill button with teal gradient and glow halo; leads conversions (Live Demo, Contact).
components.html: Live Demo

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Low-emphasis pill for the secondary action (Source / GitHub) beside a primary CTA.

components.name: Pill Chip
components.description: Tag / status chip; also the '● available now' availability badge.
components.css: .ds-chip{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;padding:4px 12px;background:rgba(0,140,147,0.07);border:1px solid rgba(0,140,147,0.2);border-radius:100px;font-family:'DM Sans',system-ui,sans-serif;font-size:0.72rem;letter-spacing:0.06em;text-transform:uppercase;color:#00adb5;transition:border-color 0.2s,background 0.2s}.ds-chip:hover{border-color:rgba(0,140,147,0.45);background:rgba(0,140,147,0.13)}.ds-chip-dot{width:6px;height:6px;border-radius:50%;background:#00adb5;box-shadow:0 0 0 0 rgba(0,173,181,0.7);animation:ds-pulse 2s infinite}@keyframes ds-pulse{0%,100%{box-shadow:0 0 0 0 rgba(0,173,181,0.7)}50%{box-shadow:0 0 0 5px rgba(0,173,181,0)}}

components.name: Tech Token
components.description: Denser square-cornered chip for tech-stack tokens inside project detail panels.

components.name: HUD Card
components.description: Console-navy panel framed with corner brackets and a scan line — the core content container.
components.html:
Project — 01

Rylix Estate

MERN property platform with auth, smart filters, and Midtrans payments.
components.css: .ds-card{position:relative;padding:24px;background:rgba(4,8,28,0.96);border:1px solid rgba(0,140,147,0.18);border-radius:14px;overflow:hidden;font-family:'DM Sans',system-ui,sans-serif}.ds-card .ds-c{position:absolute;width:14px;height:14px}.ds-tl{top:10px;left:10px;border-top:1px solid rgba(0,140,147,0.35);border-left:1px solid rgba(0,140,147,0.35)}.ds-tr{top:10px;right:10px;border-top:1px solid rgba(0,140,147,0.35);border-right:1px solid rgba(0,140,147,0.35)}.ds-bl{bottom:10px;left:10px;border-bottom:1px solid rgba(0,140,147,0.35);border-left:1px solid rgba(0,140,147,0.35)}.ds-br{bottom:10px;right:10px;border-bottom:1px solid rgba(0,140,147,0.35);border-right:1px solid rgba(0,140,147,0.35)}.ds-scan{position:absolute;left:0;right:0;height:1px;background:linear-gradient(90deg,transparent,rgba(0,140,147,0.5),transparent);animation:ds-scan 6s ease-in-out infinite;pointer-events:none}@keyframes ds-scan{0%{top:0;opacity:0}6%{opacity:1}94%{opacity:1}100%{top:100%;opacity:0}}.ds-card-eyebrow{font-family:ui-monospace,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;font-size:0.62rem;letter-spacing:0.16em;text-transform:uppercase;color:rgba(0,173,181,0.6);margin-bottom:8px}.ds-card-title{font-family:'Syne',sans-serif;font-weight:800;font-size:1.35rem;letter-spacing:-0.03em;color:#f0f4ff;margin:0 0 10px}.ds-card-body{font-size:0.84rem;line-height:1.7;color:rgba(220,230,255,0.5);margin:0}

components.name: Pill Input
components.description: Single-line field; border shifts to solid teal on focus (no glow ring).

components.name: Floating Nav
components.description: Condensed floating pill nav with gradient brand mark and teal active state.
components.html:
Rian.dev
HomeProjectsContact

narrative.overview: A developer's heads-up display made cinematic. The portfolio renders the instrument an engineer would build for themselves — corner brackets that frame every viewport, a scan line that sweeps on a slow loop, outlined display type, and live counters. The medium is the message: precision and technical confidence are demonstrated in the craft of the interface, not claimed in copy. The world is dark-first and screen-lit: near-black grounds let a single teal accent carry the surface as luminous edges and glows. Syne display slabs carry the drama; DM Sans stays quiet for reading; a system monospace speaks wherever the machine reports. Motion is deliberate and always-on — the system intentionally ignores prefers-reduced-motion because the animation is the identity — while readability and contrast are engineered to survive it.
keyCharacteristics: Dark-first, screen-lit: near-black grounds, one teal accent carried as glow and luminous edge.
Instrument furniture: corner brackets, scan lines, pulse dots, and counters as recurring HUD vocabulary.
Outlined + solid display type in tight uppercase Syne slabs.
Monospace as the machine's voice — terminal, labels, readouts — never as generic 'developer' costume.
Motion is a constant; contrast and hierarchy are built to hold up under it.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Teal is the only chromatic accent on screen at a time. Everything colored is teal or its deep stop. Switch the whole variant in index.css rather than adding a second hue.

rules.name: The No-Gray-Line Rule
rules.body: Borders, dividers, and hairlines are always the accent at low alpha, never neutral gray. A gray line breaks the screen-lit illusion.

rules.name: The Blue-White Rule
rules.body: Light text on dark is #f0f4ff (blue-cast near-white), never pure #ffffff.

rules.name: The Stencil Rule
rules.body: Display and headline slabs pair a solid line with an outlined (-webkit-text-stroke) line. The outline is the HUD signature.

rules.name: The Mono-Means-Machine Rule
rules.body: Monospace is reserved for text the system emits — terminal, readouts, labels, coordinates. Never for headlines or body prose.

rules.name: The Glow-Not-Drop Rule
rules.body: Elevation is emitted light (0 0 Npx teal halos, radial blooms), not cast shadow. The scrolled navbar is the only drop-shadow exception.

dos: Do keep teal as the single accent (#00adb5 dark / #14b8a6 light), carried as glow, border, chip, and counter.
Do ground text on dark in #f0f4ff, never pure white; keep it above 4.5:1 so it holds under the always-on motion.
Do pair a solid display line with an outlined (-webkit-text-stroke) line for the Stencil signature.
Do reserve monospace for the machine's voice — terminal, labels, counters, coordinates.
Do convey depth with emitted glow, and draw every hairline in low-alpha accent, not gray.
Do frame major viewports with the HUD kit — corner brackets, scan line, pulse dot.
Do keep every button, chip, and input on the 100px pill (textarea excepted at 16px).
donts: Don't ship the generic one-page developer template — the interchangeable, forgettable portfolio is the thing to beat.
Don't over-design into noise: motion and effects must never bury the projects or drop body contrast below legible.
Don't drift into the AI-generated look — no decorative multi-hue gradients, no glassmorphism-by-default, no gradient text (the 'Rian.dev' mark is the one sanctioned exception).
Don't use a gray border, divider, or hairline anywhere; use low-alpha teal.
Don't use a dark offset drop shadow for elevation; use a teal glow. The scrolled navbar is the only exception.
Don't use monospace as generic 'developer' decoration, or set headlines/body in it.
Don't introduce a second decorative accent hue alongside teal.
Don't nest cards, or add a colored side-stripe border to a card or callout.
